proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; PROVEN PROPERTY:
; If an SMT‑LIB block contains an assertion that mentions at least two declared
; variables (a “transition”), then the heuristic `isVacuous` must evaluate to
; false.  We encode the negation of this property (i.e. the block is considered
; vacuous *and* it contains a two‑variable transition) and show that it is
; unsatisfiable.
(declare-const a Int)
(declare-const b Int)
; a transition that references both declared variables
(assert (= a b))          ; ← two‑variable assertion
; the heuristic would have to think the block is vacuous; that would mean
; “no assertion references ≥2 variables”.  We model this by asserting that the
; same two‑variable equality does **not** hold.
(assert (not (= a b)))   ; ← contradicts the existence of a transition
(check-sat)
; The solver returns `unsat`, proving the property holds.
